channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Overhanging tree from front garden blocking pathway, 26th June
item.description:
Tree has substantially overgrown to the point that you have to walk in the road due to not being able to walk on the pathway.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Edwards Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Massive fly tipping mess at communal bins!, 26th June
item.description:
Tonnes of items and boxes dumped around both sets of communal bins at the end of the road. Needs to be cleared immediately!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Prout Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 1)Stop line faded on A, B, C- required repainting 2)Pedestrian stud faded on D phase- required repainting, 26th June
item.description:
1)Stop line faded on A, B, C- required repainting 2)Pedestrian stud faded on D phase- required repainting
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Watermead Way, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pole has been taken out, 25th June
item.description:
The pole has been taken out , causing some vehicles drive on the green area and it's very dangerous for the kids playing on green area

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 105, Ravensbury Road, St Mary Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 sets of parking restrictions painted over. Redcar Street, 25th June
item.description:
2 sets of parking restrictions spray painted over in black paint. Redcar Street canberwell. If this is not a fix my street case please state so and pass on the relavent details of the correct department. Kind regards.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Redcar Street, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins overflowing again, 25th June
item.description:
Unfortunately the bins and surrounding rubbish don’t get collected at the same pace they are compiled.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown Public Tree needs pruning, near bus stop "K" Matfield Close, 25th June
item.description:
As per fix my street report reference made approximately 10 months ago: FixMyStreet ref: 8073814, the issue was not dealt with therefore the problem and danger has increased since then due to tree growth. Tree next 1 Mapleton Close is overhanging over public pathway leading to bus stop, over bus stop itself and public pathway leading into close of residential housing. The tree is situated alongside a public footpath and close to a bus stop that is regularly used by pedestrians, including children, elderly individuals, and other vulnerable members of the community. The excessive growth of the tree has resulted in large branches extending over areas frequently used by the public. In adverse weather conditions, such as strong winds, heavy rain, or snow, there is an increased risk of branches breaking or falling, which could result in serious injury to pedestrians or damage to public infrastructure. In addition, the tree has extended substantially over the boundary of Property No. 1. Large branches now overhang the residential property, creating a foreseeable risk to the occupants and the building itself. Should a branch fail due to weather conditions, age, disease, or excessive weight, it could cause significant damage to the roof, windows, vehicles, or other parts of the property. More importantly, there is a risk of injury to residents, visitors, or anyone occupying the property at the time of such an incident. Pruning the tree would not only reduce these risks but would also constitute a proactive and responsible measure to prevent avoidable accidents, property damage, and potential liability issues. As per the last report on the same issue 10 months ago, no actions has been taken. The tree and the problem has grown.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Barnhill Avenue, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: High Street SE20 7EU (Penge) number 144 (vape, 25th June
item.description:
High Street SE20 7EU (Penge) number 144 (vape shop I think) plain wooden side (facing next door butcher) graffiti tagged.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 144, High Street, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Deep hole due to broken pavers, ? Skip lorry causing damage.

item.title: Crystal Palace Park Road SE26 flyposters, 25th June
item.description:
Crystal Palace Park Road SE26 / High Street junction. Between Thicket Road junction and pedestrian crossing (traffic lights) couple of green boxes – one of said boxes has a number of A4 size flyposters on it.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): High Street, Anerley, Greater London

item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brampton Road, West Heath, Greater London

item.title: This public tree is a nuisance, 25th June
item.description:
Needs pruning, shedding everywhere, concerned it’s close to my house, branches falling all the time. Please advise!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Queen's Mead Road, Shortlands, Greater London

item.title: Outside end of terrace, 25th June
item.description:
Fly tipping yet again.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): St Julians Road, London

item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load

item.title: Crystal Court SE19 plastic grit bin, 25th June
item.description:
Crystal Court SE19 / College Road junction is a green plastic grit bin which is graffiti tagged. Google Streetview is (noting Crystal Court sign): https://www.google.com/maps/@51.425383,-0.0760417,3a,23.5y,320.86h,84.1t/data=!3m7!1e1!3m5!1sdWAc2mUYG5qJevRndx1S_g!2e0!6shttps:%2F%2Fstreetviewpixels-pa.googleapis.com%2Fv1%2Fthumbnail%3Fcb_client%3Dmaps_sv.tactile%26w%3D900%26h%3D600%26pitch%3D5.903573475332593%26panoid%3DdWAc2mUYG5qJevRndx1S_g%26yaw%3D320.86207683940097!7i16384!8i8192?entry=ttu&g_ep=EgoyMDI2MDYyMy4wIKXMDSoASAFQAw%3D%3D
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crystal Court, Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Two abandoned cars, 25th June
item.description:
Two abandoned cars one had no tax or mot The other has no tax but has got mot Cars have been there for months the owner of the vehicles is unknown to the local community.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lincoln Road, North End, Greater London
